endpoints_dissolved.context: USER-FLAGGED: 'Like [[dark-vassal-trio]], [[wrath-of-the-dragon-saga]], [[eld-cinderella-cycle]], [[dsk-unlucky-lands-cycle]], [[fear-of-cycle]], and [[castform-forms-cycle]], this is a designer-coordinated cycle entity hosted on the character layer per the Endriders precedent.'
endpoints_dissolved.rationale: the exact 'Like [[...]]' peer-listing template-citation pattern from the wave-124 sweep. Six independent designer-coordinated cycles citing each other for architectural-template reasons only. Castform forms (Pokemon weather cycle), Eld Cinderella (MTG fairy-tale cycle), DSK Unlucky Lands (MTG horror cycle), Fear of cycle (MTG mechanic cycle), Dark Vassal (DBZ Piccolo spawn cycle), Hirudegarn saga (DBZ saga-tag cycle) — none are thematically connected to DBZ Cell-Saga apparatus content. The 'per the Endriders precedent' phrase is the schema-trigger giveaway. Six independent cycles SHOULD NOT graph-edge each other just because they share the cycle-node template.

rule_codified.name: Cycle-node-as-template-citation discipline (extends bbl-wikilink-vs-backtick-discipline)
rule_codified.statement: Designer-coordinated cycle nodes (cells of cards-grouped-by-Bandai-saga-tag, MTG-cycle-mechanic, Pokemon-form-rotation, etc.) hosted on the character layer are independent of each other. Two cycle nodes share the character-layer-hosting TEMPLATE but DO NOT share content. When citing another cycle node as a structural precedent ('Like X, this is a designer-coordinated cycle entity hosted on the character layer per the Endriders precedent'), use backticks not wikilinks. The Endriders precedent itself is a template not a thematic anchor. Cross-cycle wikilinks should exist ONLY when the cycles share canonical thematic substrate (e.g. two Pokemon weather cycles, two MTG faction cycles, two DBZ-saga cycles within the same arc).
rule_codified.applies_to: all cycle-node bodies; specifically the 6 cycle nodes in the dissolved L85 enumeration (dark-vassal-trio, wrath-of-the-dragon-saga, eld-cinderella-cycle, dsk-unlucky-lands-cycle, fear-of-cycle, castform-forms-cycle) — these 6 are independent cycle entities and should not graph-edge each other.
rule_codified.doctrinal_anchor: bbl-wikilink-vs-backtick-discipline (parent rule); wave-124 nicol-bolas sweep (analogous precedent for single-character-node template-citation pattern).

next_pass_suggestions: Audit the OTHER 5 cycle nodes (dark-vassal-trio, wrath-of-the-dragon-saga, eld-cinderella-cycle, dsk-unlucky-lands-cycle, fear-of-cycle, castform-forms-cycle) for symmetric schema-precedent wikilinks pointing back at cell-saga-apparatus and at each other. Likely the same 'Like [[...]]' peer-listing pattern was propagated. A follow-up Edgelord-dissolution pass should sweep all 6 in parallel using the same heuristic.

Consider productionizing the wave-124 janny_bolas_schema_precedent_sweep.py script as a general-purpose cycle-node-template-citation sweep (parameterized by cycle-node-slug list), to run as a one-shot janny against the full character-layer corpus.

Audit son-gohan.md, namekian.md, phyrexia.md, macro-cosmos.md for symmetric back-edges to cell-saga-apparatus — these SHOULD exist as wikilinks (real thematic edges). If missing, mirror-sync follow-up.
comic_book_guy_note: Worst graph-edge ever. Six independent cycle nodes peer-listing each other because they share a hosting template — that is a SCHEMA EDGE not a CONTENT EDGE. The wave-124 sweep already established the rule; this is the same disease, character-layer cycle-node sub-strain. User caught it via the mobile graph view, which is now the second canonical pattern-detection surface (after the first wave-124 case). Backtick discipline holds. The Endriders precedent does not entitle two unrelated cycles to a wikilink.
